data.title: Bioretec Ltd's Financial Statements 2024 published
data.description:
Bioretec Ltd's Financial Statements 2024 published

Bioretec Ltd's Financial Statements 2024 published

PR Newswire

TAMPERE, Finland, Feb. 14, 2025

Bioretec Ltd    Company announcement      14 February 2025 at 5:00 p.m. EET

TAMPERE, Finland, Feb. 14, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Bioretec Ltd has today published its 2024 Financial Statements and Report by the Board of Directors. The publications are attached to this release and also available in Finnish and English on the company's website at www.bioretec.com/investors/investors-in-english/reports-and-presentations.

Further enquiries

Alan Donze, CEO, +1 619 977 5285
Johanna Salko, CFO, +358 40 754 8172

Certified adviser 

Nordic Certified Adviser AB, +46 70 551 67 29

Bioretec in brief

Bioretec is a globally operating Finnish medical device company that continues to pioneer the application of absorbable orthopedic implants. The company has built unique competencies in the biological interface of active implants to enhance bone growth and accelerate fracture healing after orthopedic surgery. The products developed and manufactured by Bioretec are used worldwide in approximately 40 countries. 

Bioretec is developing the new RemeOs™ product line based on a magnesium alloy and hybrid composite, introducing a new generation of strong absorbable materials for enhanced surgical outcomes. The RemeOs™ implants are absorbed and replaced by bone, which eliminates the need for removal surgery while facilitating fracture healing. The combination has the potential to make titanium implants redundant and help clinics reach their Value-Based Healthcare targets while focusing on value for patients through efficient healthcare. The first RemeOs™ product market authorization has been received in the U.S. in March 2023, and in Europe, the CE mark approval was received in January 2025. Bioretec is positioning itself to enter the addressable over USD 9 billion global orthopedic trauma and spine market and to become a game changer in surgical bone fracture treatment.

Better healing – Better life. www.bioretec.com

This information was brought to you by Cision http://news.cision.com

https://news.cision.com/bioretec/r/bioretec-ltd-s-financial-statements-2024-published,c4106148

The following files are available for download:

https://mb.cision.com/Main/20509/4106148/3267007.pdf

data.title: Moderna is counting on its product pipeline to resuscitate its ailing stock
data.description:
By Ciara Linnane

COVID-19 vaccine sales topped consensus, but RSV vaccine sales remain modest

Moderna Inc.'s stock erased early losses to trade up 1.2% Friday, after the biotech company posted a wider-than-expected fourth-quarter loss and offered soft guidance for 2025, offsetting a revenue beat.

Cambridge, Mass.-based Moderna (MRNA) had a net loss of $1.1 billion for the quarter, or $2.91 a share, after earnings of $217 million, or 55 cents a share, in the year-earlier period.

Revenue fell to $1 billion from $2.8 billion a year ago. The FactSet consensus was for a loss of $2.68 per share and revenue of $943 million.

The decline in sales was mostly due to the earlier launch of the updated COVID-19 vaccine in the U.S., which shifted sales into the third quarter.

"The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approval granted three weeks earlier than in the previous year enabled the company to meet demand more effectively ahead of the fourth quarter," the company said in a statement.

The COVID vaccine Spikevax is one of Moderna's two approved products, along with mRESVIA, a vaccine for respiratory syncytial virus, or RSV.

Sales of Spikevax came to $923 million, to account for most of the quarterly total, while sales of mRESVIA came to $15 million. The Spikevax number was ahead of the FactSet consensus of $909 million but is far below peak sales from the pandemic era.

The company started marketing mRESVIA in the third quarter and achieved full-year sales of $25 million. The market is somewhat limited by the approval, which is for U.S. adults 60 and older and not for younger patients, although the company is seeking approval for high-risk adults ages 18 to 59.

The RSV vaccine has also won approvals in Canada, the E.U., the United Arab Emirates, Iceland, Qatar, Taiwan and Liechtenstein.

The company is now expecting 2025 revenue to range from $1.5 billion to $2.5 billion, in line with guidance offered in early January, with a midpoint of $2 billion, while FactSet is expecting $2.4 billion. Most of that will come in the second half, given the seasonality of the company's respiratory business.

"In 2025, we remain focused on driving sales, delivering up to 10 product approvals through 2027, and expanding cost efficiencies across our business," Chief Executive Stéphane Bancel said in prepared remarks.

On a call with analysts, Bancel said the 10 products represent a total addressable market of more than $30 billion. The company will also work on its cost structure to ensure it is breakeven on a cash cost basis by no later than 2028, he said.

The company is expecting to achieve multiple approvals starting this year once it begins to offer Phase 3 data readouts, and it is aiming to eliminate almost $1 billion in costs.

Research and development costs fell 20% in the quarter to $1.1 billion. That was mostly due to lower costs related to the COVID, RSV, seasonal-flu and combination-vaccine programs, and was partially offset by higher spending on norovirus and individualized-neoantigen-therapy (INT) programs.

Beyond its respiratory portfolio, Moderna's pipeline includes a treatment for cytomegalovirus, a virus that is particularly risky for pregnant women, because it can be passed on to the fetus and cause symptoms that include low birth weight and premature birth, enlarged liver and seizures.

A Phase 3 trial is fully enrolled, with data expected this year.

The company is also developing a vaccine against norovirus, with a Phase 3 trial dependent on case accruals. The trial is fully enrolled in the northern hemisphere, but Moderna is preparing for a second-season enrollment in the southern hemisphere.

In oncology, Moderna is working on individualized neoantigen therapy, which tailors treatment to each patient's individual cancer. The company is working with Merck and its blockbuster cancer drug Keytruda on treatments for melanoma and non-small-cell lung cancer, with trials in various stages of enrollment. It's also working on a study of an INT for invasive bladder cancer and kidney cancer.

The company's rare-disease portfolio includes treatments for propionic acidemia, or PA, and methylmalonic acidemia, or MAA, rare metabolic disorders that affect a body's ability to break down certain proteins and fats.

In January, Moderna won a U.S. Department of Health and Human Services award of $211 million to enhance mRNA platform capabilities to help the U.S. better respond to emerging infectious diseases such as bird flu.

See also: Moderna's stock is falling. Goldman Sachs says it's no longer a buy.

There is no approved vaccine for bird flu in humans, because there haven't been enough cases in the U.S. for a full clinical trial. The national stockpile includes certain shots developed in the past and tested on volunteers.

Moderna is now expecting 2025 research and development costs of about $4.1 billion and selling, general and administrative costs of about $1.1 billion. Cost of sales is expected to come to about $1.2 billion.

Moderna's stock has fallen 62% in the last 12 months, while the S&P 500 SPX has gained 22.5%.

-Ciara Linnane

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  02-14-25 1034ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: U.S. industrial output picks up - a sign that tariff threats are boosting demand for U.S. factory goods
data.description:
By Greg Robb

Cold weather also boosts utility output in January

The numbers: U.S. Industrial production rose 0.5% in January, the Federal Reserve reported Friday.

The gain was above expectations of a 0.3% rise, according to a survey by The Wall Street Journal. It follows a strong 1% gain in December.

Production is up 2% over the past year.

Capacity utilization rose to 77.8% from 77.5% in the prior month.

Economists had forecast a 77.7% rate.

The capacity-utilization rate reflects the limits to operating the nation's factories, mines and utilities.

Key details: Some of the strength was misleading as it was tied to cold weather, which led utility output to rise 7.2% in January.

The end of last year's machinists' strike at Boeing also boosted the data with production of aerospace parts rising 6.3%.

Mining output, which includes oil and natural gas, fell 1.2% after a 2% rise in the prior month.

Manufacturing alone slipped 0.1% in January after a 0.5% rise in the prior month.

Motor vehicles and parts output fell 5.2% after a 1.6% drop in the prior month. Excluding autos, total industrial output jumped 1%.

Big picture: The trend in production has been flat for two years. Economists see signs of stirring in the sector but global-trade tensions are a wild card.

"There is some sign that surveys suggest the threat of tariffs has supported demand for U.S. factory goods, said Samuel Tombs, chief U.S. economist at Pantheon Macroeconomics.

Looking ahead: "This recent uptick in production suggests that we mark up our expectations for GDP growth, although we have to see if this surge in production persists," said Carl Weinberg, chief economist at High Frequency Economics.

Market reaction: Stocks DJIA opened higher on Friday while the 10-year Treasury yield BX:TMUBMUSD10Y was down to 4.48% in early morning trading.

-Greg Robb

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  02-14-25 1023ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: U.S. Business Inventories Dipped in December
data.description:
By Ed Frankl 




  Business inventories declined in the U.S. in December, with indications of the time to clear stocks falling too, according to monthly Commerce Department data published Friday. 



  Manufacturers' and trade inventories fell 0.2% on month in December, more than offsetting the 0.1% growth in November. Economists expected a 0.1% fall, according to a poll by The Wall Street Journal. Inventories were nevertheless 2.0% above the level of December 2023. 



  The ratio of inventories to sales, which indicates how many months it would take for businesses to clear their inventories at current sales rates, was 1.35 in December, down from the 1.37 in both November 2024 and December 2023. 



  Inventories at manufacturers jumped 0.4% on month in December, while they fell 0.4% for retailers and 0.5% for merchant wholesalers, the data said. 





  Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  February 14, 2025 10:26 ET (15:26 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: NextEra Energy Raises Quarterly Dividend by 10%
data.description:
By Colin Kellaher 




  NextEra Energy's board has raised the clean-energy company's quarterly dividend by 10%, to 56.65 cents from 51.5 cents. 



  The new payout, equal to about $2.67 a year, represents an annual yield of around 3.29% based on Friday's early trading price of $68.96, up from 2.99%. 



  The Juno Beach, Fla., company said the increased dividend is payable March 17 to shareholders of record Feb. 28. 





  Write to Colin Kellaher at colin.kellaher@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  February 14, 2025 10:12 ET (15:12 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: From Ambition to Icon: Pressence 360 Transforms Dubai Realtors Into Luxury Real Estate Powerhouses
data.description:
Dubai-Based Agency Redefines Success Through Strategic Branding and Visibility

DUBAI, UAE / ACCESS Newswire / February 14, 2025 / In Dubai's high-stakes luxury real estate market, where competition thrives amid towering skyscrapers and opulent lifestyles, a new wave of agents is rising to prominence. Pressence 360, a boutique marketing agency, is behind this shift, turning realtors into household names through innovative branding and media strategies.

The Art of Building Real Estate Legends

Pressence 360's approach transcends traditional property marketing. Instead, the agency focuses on transforming agents into recognizable brands. By blending viral social media campaigns, high-profile media placements, and cinematic storytelling, they've earned a reputation as the architects behind Dubai's most visible real estate professionals.

"Our mission isn't just to sell properties-it's to make our clients synonymous with luxury living," says Soumadeep Mondal, Co-Founder of Pressence 360. "When buyers think of Dubai's elite market, we want them to think of our partners first."

A Blueprint for Visibility

  • The agency's tailored strategies have delivered measurable results for agents competing in the city's crowded landscape:

  • Organic Social Mastery: Clients average 3 million annual views across Instagram and TikTok through content designed to resonate with global audiences.

  • Media Dominance: Securing features in Forbes, GQ Middle East, and Rolling Stone MENA, alongside expedited social media verification, positions agents as industry authorities.

  • Cinematic Storytelling: High-end production crews capture agents in 4K, whether showcasing Palm Jumeirah estates or networking at Burj Khalifa hotspots.

  • Rapid Transformation: Within 28 days, agents evolve from local players to sought-after experts, commanding premium fees and attracting high-net-worth clientele.

Founders with a Fighter's Mentality

Pressence 360's leadership reflects the tenacity they instill in clients. Co-Founder Soumadeep Mondal, a self-made entrepreneur who launched a seven-figure marketing agency at 19, leverages his Delhi upbringing to identify driven talent. "Dubai's top agents are outliers," he notes. "They just need the right platform to shine."

Austin Clark, Co-Founder and former Muay Thai champion, channels his combat sports discipline into marketing.

"Real estate is about owning the narrative," Clark says. "We teach agents to stop competing and start leading."

Proven Impact in a Cutthroat Market

Recent successes include a client closing a $28 million villa sale days after a Forbes profile and another tripling inquiries post-verification. In a market where visibility dictates survival, Pressence 360's strategies are game-changers.

Join the New Era of Real Estate

Pressence 360 is selectively partnering with agents ready to transcend transactions and become cultural influencers. Eligibility hinges on ambition: the drive to attract international clients, command unparalleled fees, and leave a legacy beyond listings.

"The future isn't in listings-it's in influence," Clark emphasizes. "We're here to give agents the tools to own it."

About Pressence 360

Pressence 360 is a Dubai-based marketing agency specializing in personal branding for luxury real estate professionals. By combining media relations, social strategy, and premium content, they empower agents to dominate markets and redefine industry standards.

Media Contact:

Name: Pressence 360
Phone: +44 7876853854
Email: contactus@pressence360.com
Website: https://pressence360.com/

SOURCE: Pressence 360

data.title: INTRODUCING SIMPLE THEORY WINE CO: MILLENNIAL AND GEN Z COUSINS MAKING EUROPEAN VARIETAL WINES LAUNCH NEW WINERY AND TASTING LOUNGE IN MARYLAND WINE COUNTRY
data.description:
INTRODUCING SIMPLE THEORY WINE CO: MILLENNIAL AND GEN Z COUSINS MAKING EUROPEAN VARIETAL WINES LAUNCH NEW WINERY AND TASTING LOUNGE IN MARYLAND WINE COUNTRY

INTRODUCING SIMPLE THEORY WINE CO: MILLENNIAL AND GEN Z COUSINS MAKING EUROPEAN VARIETAL WINES LAUNCH NEW WINERY AND TASTING LOUNGE IN MARYLAND WINE COUNTRY

PR Newswire

FREDERICK, Md., Feb. 14, 2025

Third-Generation Grower-Vintners Rise to The Challenges and Opportunities of a Changing Climate

FREDERICK, Md., Feb. 14, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Today marks the official launch of Maryland's Simple Theory Wine Co – SimpleTheoryWines.com – dedicated to growing and crafting wines primarily from traditional, European Vitis vinifera grape varieties, in response to the region's changing climate.

Backed by more than 50 years of family farming and winemaking leadership, the winery is being launched by vintner Melissa Aellen, 33, along with her cousin, focused on vineyard management, Parker Aellen, 24. The initial releases, including Chardonnay, Petit Verdot, Barbera, Albariño, Merlot and more, offer a refined, contemporary collection of estategrown wines that blend the art and science of winemaking with a deep commitment to sustainability. Wines range in price from $28 to $49.

Simple Theory's groundbreaking opportunity comes as Maryland's increasingly mild winters and warmer, drier summers have enabled the previously unlikely ripening of traditional European grape varieties.

"Everyone hears about climate change, but I find it most commonly seen by and impacts those who work with the land as my family has for decades," says Melissa Aellen. "We can grow grapes now that we couldn't ripen 30 years ago. The climate has changed that much."

Simple Theory Tasting Lounge

To bring the vision of Simple Theory fully to life, Melissa Aellen and her family have opened the Simple Theory Wine Co Tasting Lounge offering a relaxed, contemporary space for tasting wines in the heart of Frederick, MD, a vibrant center of food, wine, art, and culture about an hour's drive from Washington, DC, and Baltimore.

"We are thrilled to open Frederick's only winery tasting lounge, serving as the perfect way to learn about our wines and share our commitment to sustainable farming, winemaking, and ideally becoming a community gathering place for residents and visitors alike," says Melissa.

Expanding a 50-Year Family Legacy

Based in Frederick, Maryland, located 40 miles north of Washington, D.C., Simple Theory Wine Co is built on the insights and strategies gained from a family with more than 50 years of experience as Maryland winegrowers.

The Aellen family's legacy began in 1972 when Melissa's and Parker's grandparents first established wine grapes in nearby Mt. Airy, MD. In 1983, recognizing the growing interest in regionally produced wines, they worked to establish the first federally designated grape growing area in Maryland, the Linganore American Viticultural Area (AVA), which encompasses 90 square miles of land around the winery in the heart of Maryland. Today, Melissa and Parker carry this legacy forward, re-thinking the vineyards and the wine business with a fresh vision that embraces both tradition and modernity.

"I've wanted to be a winemaker and work with my dad since I was 10 when my grandmother showed me a picture of Gina Gallo on the cover of a magazine, which is when I first realized women could be winemakers," says Melissa Aellen. "This is all I've ever wanted to do."

Aellen went on to earn a degree in winemaking and viticulture from Cornell University in 2013 and further honed her craft at wineries in Oregon, Illinois, New York's Finger Lakes, New Zealand, and Napa Valley before returning to her roots in Maryland wine country in 2017. Along the way she also earned an Executive MBA.

Estate-Grown and Sustainable

Simple Theory wines are produced from twenty-five acres planted to Pinot Gris, Chardonnay, Merlot, Malbec, Cabernet Franc, and Cabernet Sauvignon among others. Plans call for vineyard expansion in 2025 with the planting of a newly-acquired 41-acre site.

The in-house farming team follows a regimen of integrated pest management, including use of wide cover crop rows to create insect habitats that reduce reliance on herbicides. Spray schedules are calibrated based on need, rather than a pre-assigned schedule, allowing for surgical use of applications. Minimal mowing also promotes healthier bug populations which, in turn, drive a healthier and more biodiverse ecosystem in the vineyards. The vineyards are dry farmed.

The production facility is 100% solar-powered and has been honored with a Maryland Green Business of the Year Award.

"We are working toward becoming a certified B Corp where sustainable initiatives reach beyond the environment and encompass the community and the winery's employees as well. Our environmental efforts are designed to preserve our business for the next 50 years," says Aellen.

About Simple Theory Wine Co.

Simple Theory Wine Co is a boutique winery located in Frederick, Maryland. Founded by third-generation vintner Melissa Aellen and her family, the winery combines a rich heritage of Maryland winegrowing with a forward-looking approach to sustainability and innovation. Simple Theory's collection of estate-grown wines showcases the best of both classic and hybrid grape varieties, reflecting the unique terroir of Maryland's evolving climate.

Simple Theory Wine Co
www.SimpleTheoryWines.Com
301-969-4446
Tasting Lounge: 37 N Market St, Suite 200, Frederick, MD 21701

SOURCE Simple Theory Wine Co

data.title: ICE Canola Finds Higher Ground
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--The ICE Futures canola market rebounded on Friday morning to go with positive sentiment in other markets.

Chicago soyoil, European rapeseed and Malaysian palm oil were all higher. Crude oil also made gains after United States President Donald Trump did not immediately place tariffs on trading partners.

The Canadian dollar was up four-tenths of a U.S. cent compared to Thursday's close.

Nearly 19,200 contracts were traded. Prices in Canadian dollars per metric ton as of 8:37 CST: 

 
           Price      Change 
Mar.      664.30     up 5.70 
May       674.30     up 6.70 
Jul.      678.60     up 6.40 
Nov.      653.50     up 6.30

data.title: Freshii Introduces New Ovens as Part of Commitment to Delivering Healthy Foods, Bold Taste and Expanded Menu Fast and Freshly
data.description:
Freshii Introduces New Ovens as Part of Commitment to Delivering Healthy Foods, Bold Taste and Expanded Menu Fast and Freshly

Freshii, the go-to restaurant for fast, fresh, flavourful meals on the go, announces today the beginning of a rollout of cutting-edge ovens across its locations. This investment allows Freshii locations to expand their menus, offering even more options for guests who are looking for flavour, nutrition and value.

This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250214744316/en/

Freshii begins the rollout of industry-leading ovens by Amana to help it deliver delicious food that is consistent and fresh, while enjoying greater efficiency in the kitchen for even faster service. PHOTO: Foodtastic

The new ovens reflect Freshii’s commitment to exceptional quality, taste and menu innovation. Supplied by Amana, the ovens introduce game-changing technology to a growing number of Freshii's locations. Foodtastic, the foodservice leader that acquired Freshii in 2023, expects a rollout across the majority of its locations by the summer, with more to follow. Once installed, the ovens give each restaurant the highest level of versatility and precision available on the market to prepare Freshii’s core menu items like oven-roasted chicken, and to expand into new options like roasted vegetables and salmon. The ovens will not only help Freshii’s locations deliver on delicious food that is consistent and fresh, but also improve efficiency in the kitchen, ensuring even faster service.

“Everyone deserves fresh and nutritious food that tastes amazing,” said Chris Cann, Brand Leader at Freshii. “We have invested wholeheartedly in Freshii because we believe in the brand’s promise, and we love the food. This rollout marks an important step in returning to basics, offering fresh and wholesome meal options with bold flavour that leave our customers wanting and feeling happy to return for more. These ovens are an investment in our future and our commitment to empowering people to live their best lives."

The oven rollout is part of a new journey for Freshii – one that is more focused than ever on helping customers live happier, well-balanced lives through easy access to fresh and tasty food, anytime, anywhere.

Learn more at freshii.com.

About Freshii

Freshii is a quick-service restaurant that provides health-conscious consumers with fresh, nutritious, and flavourful meal options on the go. With a vision of creating a world where no one is forced to compromise on fresh and healthy food, Freshii empowers people to live their best lives through access to wholesome and delicious meals anytime, anywhere. Freshii is owned by Foodtastic, one of the largest restaurant franchise companies in Canada with a portfolio of restaurants that includes Milestones, Pita Pit, Quesada, Second Cup and at least 22 other renowned banners. For additional information, visit freshii.com and foodtastic.ca.

For more information, additional photos or to schedule an interview:
Mary Moniz
Torchia Communications
torchiacom.com
647-278-0152
mary@torchiacom.com
